rationale: The "relaxing bus-sort puzzle" hook is fundamentally misaligned with Push'em All's energetic, competitive crowd-pushing action loop — selling calm/relaxation undermines the satisfying-aggression fantasy that drives this game's installs. Visual frictions are significant too: the archetype's purple/orange puzzle-board palette and stylus-on-2D-grid framing clash with the game's vibrant blue/red 3D stickman platforms and there's no character protagonist to anchor the player. Audience overlaps modestly on the casual hypercasual layer, but we'd recommend skipping this archetype rather than retrofitting it; if forced, only the "ASMR satisfaction" emotional beat is transferable — re-shoot with stickmen tumbling off platforms in a satisfying chain.
